title: API: images prune returns different schema and `null` instead of empty array
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

This affects version 3.4.x, I have not tested the development version 4.x since the project I'm working on is targeting 3.4.x and 4.x will contain breaking API changes.

The images prune API endpoint returns a different schema than the reference describes.
When nothing can be pruned, `null` is returned instead of an empty Array.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. ``podman system service unix:///home/`whoami`/testing.sock --log-level=debug --time=500``

2. ``curl -X POST --unix-socket "/home/`whoami`/testing.sock" "http://d/v3.2.0/libpod/images/prune"``

**Describe the results you received:**
When something can be pruned:
```
[
   {
      "Id":"9cbc6de5bae3c3f326432444bb789edf142c5c67288d0c78e1836c19bf3aa45d",
      "Err":null,
      "Size":5870890
   },
   {
      "Id":"7ecbe8775bc3e80289cb74f1a6ce92275f6772086cfbb80a67615d525c7d1265",
      "Err":null,
      "Size":2901591
   }
]
```

When nothing can be pruned:
`null`


**Describe the results you expected:**
When something can be pruned:
```
[
   {
      "deleted":"string",
      "untagged":[
         "string"
      ]
   }
]
```

When nothing can be pruned:
`[]`


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2-dev
API Version:  3.4.2-dev
Go Version:   go1.15.9
Git Commit:   09aade7816e096550e805869f1300d7198aa8f91
Built:        Thu Oct 21 13:36:11 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes, latest version of 3.4.x built from source.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Windows 10, WSL, Debian Bullseye, 64bit

comments.body:
@jwhonce PTAL
@arctic-alpaca interested in opening a PR to fix?
@rhatdan Sorry, I'm not familiar with Go.

I think question here is:
Do we want to change prune endpoint to return body of shape:
```
[
   {
      "deleted":"string",
      "untagged":[
         "string"
      ]
   }
]
```
like the image delete endpoint does,  so it is consistent, but breaking API by doing it.

Or do we want to change swagger spec? @jwhonce @rhatdan wdyt?
I think for `podman` 4.0 we could change API, but I don't think that it can be back-ported to 3.X.
@arctic-alpaca I wonder are you to create bindings for some language other that `Go`?
@matejvasek You are correct, I'm currently working on rust bindings / API-client. At this time, it's not open source yet, but this is planned and approved by my employer.
@arctic-alpaca `podman` also serves `docker` API, so until swagger doc is fixed / you library implemented, you can use `podman` with rust docker client if there is any.
@matejvasek Thanks for the heads-up. We work with pods so we need podmans libpod API.
